name: Cervera Trails Loop
name_original: Vuelta por los senderos de Cervera — La Segarra
description: This 24.9-mile (40.0 km) moderate mountain biking route in Cervera, La Segarra, offers cultural exploration and scenic rural landscapes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cervera offers a network of routes through the rolling hills and agricultural landscapes of Catalonia. The region features a mix of open terrain, forested sections, and paths connecting historical sites. These trails provide varied elevations suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cervera The most popular mountain bike trail is Cervera Trails Loop, a 24.9 miles (40.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete, traversing varied terrain around the town. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castillo de Montcortés – Castell de l'Aranyó loop from Cervera, a moderate 29.4 miles (47.3 km) path. This route connects historical castles and offers expansive views of the surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the University of Cervera – Cervera loop from Cervera, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail leading through local landmarks and rural paths, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Cervera is defined by rolling hills, historical sites, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging routes, catering to various abilities. The routes in Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cervera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cervera?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails documented on komoot around Cervera, offering a diverse range of options for different skill levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Cervera?
A: The trails around Cervera offer a mix of difficulties. You'll find 9 easy routes, 30 moderate routes, and 4 challenging routes, catering to various experience levels.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike routes in Cervera?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for beginners or families. One option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10468501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;University of Cervera – University of Cervera loop from Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, which is 20.5 miles (33 km) long and classified as easy.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cervera?
A: Mountain biking around Cervera features rolling hills, agricultural plains, and forested sections. The region is characterized by varied terrain, often connecting historical sites and offering expansive views of the countryside.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails in Cervera?
A: The trails around Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Many mountain bikers appreciate the varied terrain, the opportunity to explore historical sites, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by historical sites or castles in Cervera?
A: Yes, many routes incorporate historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17550010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montcortés – Castell de l'Aranyó loop from Cervera&lt;/a&gt; connects two historical castles. You can also find routes that pass by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montfalcó Murallat&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de l'Aranyó&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Cervera?
A: Yes, many of the trails are designed as loops. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946132150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera Trails Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 24.9 miles (40.1 km) circular route traversing varied terrain around the town.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Cervera?
A: Cervera, located in Catalonia, generally offers good mountain biking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17550010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montcortés – Castell de l'Aranyó loop from Cervera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 29.4 miles (47.3 km) path that offers expansive views and connects historical sites. There are also 4 routes classified as difficult for those looking for a significant challenge.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can see while mountain biking in Cervera?
A: Beyond the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera&lt;/a&gt; itself, you can encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat&lt;/a&gt;, the fortified village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montfalcó Murallat&lt;/a&gt;, and various castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de l'Aranyó&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;, which offer scenic views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Cervera typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly by route and rider pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10468469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;University of Cervera – Cervera loop from Cervera&lt;/a&gt; is often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946132150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera Trails Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 36 minutes.
tips: The University of Cervera is one of the most beautiful Baroque buildings in Catalonia. Founded by King Philip V in 1717 as compensation for damages suffered during the War of Succession

On the outskirts of Cervera and in the middle of the fields, we find this church built in the 11th century and in Romanesque style, it is very well preserved and stands out for being one of the few churches with a circular nave.
Also called, Monk's Cabin. Located in the municipality of Ribera d'Ondara. We will suddenly find a set of ruins in the middle of the crop fields. A nice place to recover energy.

faqs.question: Is the Vuelta por los senderos de Cervera — La Segarra suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is rated as moderate. It requires a basic to intermediate fitness level and some prior mountain biking experience. The technical difficulty is approximately STS S2, meaning it's manageable for experienced riders but might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse landscape, traversing rural trails and mountain paths. The route also passes through extensive cultivated fields, often featuring ancient olive, holm oak, and carob trees. The natural environment includes diverse flora and crystal-clear springs, offering a rugged yet charming experience.

faqs.question: What historical and cultural sites can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in cultural heritage. You'll pass by significant sites such as the University of Cervera, Sant Pere el Gros Church, and Corral del Monjo. The area is also known for its medieval castles and historic villages, allowing you to combine physical activity with cultural exploration.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this route are typically during spring or autumn. These seasons offer more pleasant temperatures and conditions for mountain biking in the La Segarra region.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Cervera, as the starting point, offers various parking options within the town. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially near the town center or university area.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through cultivated areas, near livestock, or in populated sections of villages. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access and ride the Vuelta por los senderos de Cervera — La Segarra mountain biking route. It's a public trail accessible to all.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Camí de Sant Jaume (l'Hospitalet - Tàrrega), Via cicloturística InterCatalunya, and El Camí IT2TR3 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get supplies near the trail?
faqs.answer: Cervera, being the main town, offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can find food, drinks, and other supplies before or after your ride. Smaller villages along the route might have limited options.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate mountain bike ride like this?
faqs.answer: For this moderate mountain biking route, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, and sun protection. Given the varied terrain, sturdy mountain biking shoes are also advisable.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 40 kilometers long. Based on its moderate difficulty and typical mountain biking speeds, it generally takes around 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete, though this can vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the sights.
